figures.caption: Figure 6: Examples for two-term subtraction tasks, including the comparison of the complete thinking process of Qwen2-VL7B and our model.

Figure 1: The pipeline of IRPD dataset generation. Firstly, we extract triplets (relation : subject → object) from ConceptNet (Speer, Chin, and Havasi 2017). Secondly, we generate the images using text-to-image diffusion models, such as Flux (Labs 2024). Moreover, LLMs validation, a CLIP-based selection criterion, and human evaluation are applied to ensure the quality of subject-object pairs in the text and image modality, respectively.

Table 1: Quantitative results on the IRPD dataset and the downstream task on a real-world VQA dataset, Visual7W-telling (Zhu et al. 2016). Methods marked with * are embedding arithmetic and perform significantly worse than large vision-language models (LVLMs) with reasoning capabilities. Our approach, based on Qwen2-VL-7B and fine-tuned with SAri-RFT, achieves a relative improvement of over 50% on the two-term subtraction task (4-option) with image-based subject-object pair input q2image, and over 75% on the three-term task with q3image input, compared to standard supervised fine-tuning (SFT).
Figure 5: IRPD Dataset Examples. We show 6 relations: used for, part of, made of, antonym, created by, each with 4 examples above.

Figure 2: Examples of the IRPD Dataset. We show four relations in this figure, including “used for,” “part of,” “made of,” and “antonym.” Each line illustrates four subject-object pairs corresponding to each relation in text and image modality.
Figure 9: Examples for three-term operation task including the comparison of complete thinking process of Qwen2-VL-7B and our model with text-based question input q3text.
Figure 10: Complete prompt for subtraction and three-term operation tasks for both training and testing stages.
Figure 8: Examples for three-term operation task including the comparison of complete thinking process of Qwen2-VL-7B and our model with text-based question input q3text.
Figure 7: Examples for three-term operation task, including the comparison of the complete thinking process of Qwen2-VL-7B and our model with image prompts.
